briefdescription.para: The default size of an NMT CAN frame buffer in the absence of dynamic memory allocation.
The default maximum number of heartbeat consumers in the absence of dynamic memory allocation.

detaileddescription.para: The default value equals the maximum number of CANopen nodes.

briefdescription.para: The services enabled in the NMT 'pre-operational' state.
The services enabled in the NMT 'operational' state.
The services enabled in the NMT 'stopped' state.
An opaque CANopen NMT state type.
The 'initializing' state.
The NMT 'reset application' state.
The NMT 'reset communication' state.
The NMT 'boot-up' state.
The NMT 'pre-operational' state.
The NMT 'operational' state.
The NMT 'stopped' state.
Allocates memory for co_nmt_t object using allocator from can_net_t.
Frees memory allocated for co_nmt_t object.
Initializes co_nmt_t object.
Finalizes co_nmt_t object.
The download indication function for CANopen object 1016 (Consumer heartbeat time).
The download indication function for CANopen object 1017 (Producer heartbeat time).
The download indication function for CANopen ECSS redundancy object.
The download indication function for (all sub-objects of) CANopen object 1F80 (NMT startup).
The download indication function for (all sub-objects of) CANopen object 1F82 (Request NMT).
The CAN receive callback function for NMT messages.
The CAN receive callback function for NMT error control (node guarding RTR) messages.

detaileddescription.para: In case of an NMT master, this function also receives and processes the boot-up events (see Fig. 13 in CiA 302-2 version 4.1.0).

briefdescription.para: The CAN timer callback function for life guarding or heartbeat production.
The CAN timer callback function for sending buffered NMT messages.
The indication function for state change events.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127, 255]).
the state of the node.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason, void *data)
memberdef.name: default_hb_ind

briefdescription.para: The default heartbeat event handler.
The default state change event handler.
The Transmit-PDO event indication function.
Sets TPDO event mask for given event.
Enters the specified state of an NMT master/slave service and invokes the exit and entry functions.
Invokes the 'NMT command received' transition function of the current state of an NMT master/slave service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the NMT command specifier (one of CO_NMT_CS_START, CO_NMT_CS_STOP, CO_NMT_CS_ENTER_PREOP, CO_NMT_CS_RESET_NODE or CO_NMT_CS_RESET_COMM).

briefdescription.para: The 'NMT command received' transition function of the 'initializing' state.
The entry function of the 'reset application' state.
The entry function of the 'reset communication' state.
The entry function of the 'boot-up' state.
The 'NMT command received' transition function of the 'boot-up' state.
The entry function of the 'pre-operational' state.
The 'NMT command received' transition function of the 'pre-operational' state.
The entry function of the 'operational' state.
The 'NMT command received' transition function of the 'operational' state.
The entry function of the 'stopped' state.
The 'NMT command received' transition function of the 'stopped' state.
The NMT startup procedure (see Fig. 1 & 2 in CiA 302-2 version 4.1.0).
The NMT master startup procedure.
The NMT slave startup procedure.
Initializes the NMT redundancy manager service.
Finalizes the NMT redundancy manager service.
Gets the Redundancy Master's Node-ID and heartbeat timeout.
Initializes the NMT redundancy manager master service.
Initializes the NMT redundancy manager slave service.
Updates the state of the redundancy manager service.
Processes a heartbeat event from the Redundancy Master for a slave node.
Starts the bus selection process for a slave node.
Finishes the bus selection process for a slave node.
Initializes the error control services.
Finalizes the error control services.
Updates and (de)activates the life guarding or heartbeat production services.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the download indication functions when object 100C (Guard time), 100D (Life time factor) or 1017 (Producer heartbeat time) is updated.

briefdescription.para: Sends an NMT error control response message.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the node state and toggle bit.

briefdescription.para: Initializes the heartbeat consumer services.
Finalizes the heartbeat consumer services.
Initializes NMT slave management.
Finalizes NMT slave management.
Checks if boot-up messages have been received from all mandatory slaves.

simplesect.para: true if all boot-up messages were received, false if not.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_dev_t *const dev, const co_unsigned8_t id, const co_unsigned16_t ms)
memberdef.name: co_dev_cfg_hb

briefdescription.para: Configures heartbeat consumption for the specified node by updating CANopen object 1016 (Consumer heartbeat time).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
the heartbeat time (in milliseconds). If ms is 0, the heartbeat consumer is disabled.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a string describing an NMT boot error status.
Returns the alignment (in bytes) of the co_nmt_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of the co_nmt_t structure.
Creates a new CANopen NMT master/slave service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network.
a pointer to a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a new NMT service, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Destroys a CANopen NMT master/slave service.
Returns a pointer to the CAN network of an NMT master/slave service.
Returns a pointer to the CANopen device of an NMT master/slave service.
Retrieves the indication function invoked when an NMT command is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when an NMT command is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a heartbeat event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a heartbeat event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used (which invokes co_nmt_on_hb()).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_on_hb

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior when a heartbeat event occurs (see sections 7.2.8.2.2.3 and 7.2.8.3.2.2 in CiA 301 version 4.2.0).

detaileddescription.para: This function invokes co_nmt_node_err_ind() or co_nmt_comm_err_ind() when a timeout event occurs, depending on whether the NMT service is a master or not (see co_nmt_is_master()).

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a redundancy event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a redundancy event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked (can be NULL).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a state change is detected.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a state change is detected.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used (which invokes co_nmt_on_st()).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior when a state change is detected by the node guarding or heartbeat protocol.

detaileddescription.para: In case of a boot-up event (and the NMT service is a master), this function invokes co_nmt_boot_req() to boot the slave (see Fig. 13 in CiA 302-2).

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function used to notify the user of the progress of the current SDO download request during the CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function used to notify the user of the progress of the current SDO download request during the CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function used to notify the user of the progress of the current SDO upload request during the CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function used to notify the user of the progress of the current SDO upload request during the CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked by co_nmt_on_sync() after all PDOs have been transmitted/processed.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked by co_nmt_on_sync() after all PDOs have been transmitted/processed.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior after a SYNC object is received or transmitted.

detaileddescription.para: First all synchronous Transmit-PDOs are sent with co_tpdo_sync(). Then all synchronous Receive-PDOs are actuated with co_rpdo_sync(). Finally the user-defined callback function set with co_nmt_set_sync_ind() is invoked.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned16_t eec, co_unsigned8_t er, const co_unsigned8_t msef[5])
memberdef.name: co_nmt_on_err

briefdescription.para: Implements the default error handling behavior by generating an EMCY message with co_emcy_push() and invoking co_nmt_comm_err_ind() in case of a communication error (emergency error code 0x81xx).
Implements the default behavior when an event is indicated for an event-driven (asynchronous) Transmit-PDO by triggering the transmission of the PDO with co_tpdo_event().

detaileddescription.para: The transmission of PDOs can be postponed with co_nmt_on_tpdo_event_lock().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the PDO number (in the range [1..512]). If n is 0, the transmission of all PDOs is triggered.

briefdescription.para: Postpones the transmission of PDOs triggered by co_nmt_on_tpdo_event() until a matching call to co_nmt_on_tpdo_event_unlock().

detaileddescription.para: This function can be invoked multiple times. PDO transmission will resume after an equal number of calls to co_nmt_on_tpdo_event_unlock().

briefdescription.para: Undoes the effect of a single call to co_nmt_on_tpdo_event_lock() and possibly triggers the transmission of postponed PDOs.
Returns the pending node-ID.
Sets the pending node-ID.

detaileddescription.para: The node-ID of the device will be updated once the NMT 'reset communication command' is received. This is used for the LSS configure node-ID protocol.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the node-ID of the device (in the range [1..127, 255]).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the current state of a CANopen NMT service (one of CO_NMT_ST_BOOTUP, CO_NMT_ST_STOP, CO_NMT_ST_START, CO_NMT_ST_RESET_NODE, CO_NMT_ST_RESET_COMM or CO_NMT_ST_PREOP).
Returns true if the specified CANopen NMT service is a master, and false if not.
Sets the alternate CAN bus identifier which the NMT redundancy manager service could utilize.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a CAN bus identifier.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the currently active CAN bus on which the NMT service operates.
Sets the active CAN bus on which the NMT service operates.

detaileddescription.para: Only NMT master can initiate a bus change.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a CAN bus identifier.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the default SDO timeout used during the NMT 'boot slave' and 'check configuration' processes.
Sets the default SDO timeout used during the NMT 'boot slave' and 'check configuration' processes.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the SDO timeout (in milliseconds). See co_csdo_set_timeout().

briefdescription.para: Submits an NMT request to a slave.

detaileddescription.para: If id equals the node-ID of the master, this is equivalent to co_nmt_cs_ind(nmt, cs).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the NMT command specifier (one of CO_NMT_CS_START, CO_NMT_CS_STOP, CO_NMT_CS_ENTER_PREOP, CO_NMT_CS_RESET_NODE or CO_NMT_CS_RESET_COMM).
the node-ID (0 for all nodes, [1..127] for a specific slave).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Checks if a boot-up message has been received from the specified node(s).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (0 for all mandatory nodes, [1..127] for a specific slave).

simplesect.para: 1 if a boot-up message was received, 0 if not, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Processes an NMT command from the master or the application.

detaileddescription.para: Note that this function MAY trigger a reset of one or more CANopen services and invalidate previously obtained results of co_nmt_get_rpdo(), co_nmt_get_tpdo(), co_nmt_get_ssdo(), co_nmt_get_csdo(), co_nmt_get_sync(), co_nmt_get_time() and/or co_nmt_get_emcy().

This function MUST NOT be invoked during an NMT state transition. It is therefore unsafe to call co_nmt_cs_ind() (or co_nmt_cs_req() with the node-ID of the master) from the co_nmt_cs_ind_t and co_nmt_st_ind_t indication functions.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the NMT command specifier (one of CO_NMT_CS_START, CO_NMT_CS_STOP, CO_NMT_CS_ENTER_PREOP, CO_NMT_CS_RESET_NODE or CO_NMT_CS_RESET_COMM).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Indicates the occurrence of a communication error and invokes the specified error behavior (object 1029:01).

detaileddescription.para: Note that this function MAY invoke co_nmt_cs_ind().

briefdescription.para: Indicates the occurrence of an error event and triggers the error handling process (see Fig.

detaileddescription.para: 12 in CiA 302-2 version 4.1.0). Note that this function MAY invoke co_nmt_cs_ind().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a Receive-PDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the PDO number (in the range [1..512]).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the PDO number (in the range [1..512]).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a Server-SDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the SDO number (in the range [1..128]).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a Client-SDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the SDO number (in the range [1..128]).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the SYNC producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the TIME producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the EMCY producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the LSS master/slave service.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason, co_unsigned8_t st)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_hb_ind

briefdescription.para: The CANopen NMT heartbeat indication function, invoked when a heartbeat event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
indicates whether the event occurred (CO_NMT_EC_OCCURRED) or was resolved (CO_NMT_EC_RESOLVED). Note that heartbeat state change events only occur and are never resolved.
indicates whether the event occurred because of a timeout (CO_NMT_EC_TIMEOUT) or a state change (CO_NMT_EC_STATE).
the state of the node (if reason is CO_NMT_EC_STATE).

briefdescription.para: The CANopen NMT redundancy indication function, invoked when a redundancy event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a CAN bus identifier on which the event occurred.
indicates whether the event occurred because of a bus switch (CO_NMT_ECSS_RDN_BUS_SWITCH) or a no-master operation (CO_NMT_ECSS_RDN_NO_MASTER).

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the implementation of the network management (NMT) functions.

detaileddescription.para:
lely/co/nmt.h
2017-2021 Lely Industries N.V.
